entries.description: Establishment of the first hospitals, hospices, and systematic care for the sick, poor, orphans, and dying across the Roman Empire
entries.causation_evidence: Basil of Caesarea's hospital and early Church philanthropy records

entries.description: Central role in the abolition of the transatlantic slave trade and slavery itself (Wilberforce, Evangelical movement, Quakers, etc.)
entries.causation_evidence: British parliamentary records and evangelical abolitionist literature

entries.description: Inspiration for the Civil Rights Movement through Martin Luther King Jr. and others drawing directly from the teachings of Jesus
entries.causation_evidence: MLK speeches and writings

entries.description: Founding of the first modern universities in Europe (Oxford, Cambridge, Paris, Bologna, etc.) rooted in Christian scholarship
entries.causation_evidence: University charters and historical records

entries.description: Inspiration for the founding of the International Red Cross and global humanitarian movement
entries.causation_evidence: Henri Dunant's writings and Red Cross founding documents

entries.description: Major influence on the Universal Declaration of Human Rights through Christian concepts of human dignity
entries.causation_evidence: UDHR drafting history and Christian framers

entries.description: Global network of Christian charities, hospitals, schools, and humanitarian organizations providing aid to hundreds of millions
entries.causation_evidence: World Vision, Caritas, Samaritan's Purse, Catholic Relief Services, etc.

entries.description: Medieval Inquisition, Crusades, and centuries of religious persecution, forced conversions, and suppression of heresy
entries.causation_evidence: Historical records of the Inquisition, Crusades, and Church-sanctioned violence

entries.description: Ongoing Catholic Church sexual abuse crisis and global cover-ups spanning decades, with hundreds of thousands of victims
entries.causation_evidence: John Jay Report, CIASE (France), Australian Royal Commission, German MHG Study, and ongoing 2025 revelations

entries.description: Historical role in justifying slavery, colonialism, and the transatlantic slave trade through religious doctrine
entries.causation_evidence: Papal bulls, theological writings, and colonial records

entries.description: Witch hunts, persecution of women, and execution of tens of thousands accused of witchcraft across Europe and colonies
entries.causation_evidence: Historical trial records and scholarly estimates

entries.description: Forced conversions, cultural destruction, and violence against indigenous peoples during European colonization
entries.causation_evidence: Historical accounts of conquest in Americas, Africa, Australia, and Asia

entries.description: Anti-Semitism and theological teachings that contributed to centuries of pogroms and discrimination against Jews
entries.causation_evidence: Historical Church documents and scholarly consensus

entries.description: Sex abuse scandals and institutional cover-ups in Protestant, Orthodox, and other Christian denominations worldwide
entries.causation_evidence: Multiple independent investigations and reports 2000–2026

entries.description: Historical and ongoing opposition to scientific progress in certain branches (evolution, stem cell research, climate science, etc.)
entries.causation_evidence: Documented conflicts between fundamentalist Christianity and science

entries.description: Support for authoritarian regimes and dictatorships by major Christian institutions in the 20th and 21st centuries
entries.causation_evidence: Historical examples in Latin America, Africa, and Europe

entries.description: Exclusion, discrimination, and violence against LGBTQ individuals justified by certain Christian teachings
entries.causation_evidence: Reports on conversion therapy, legal discrimination, and violence in Christian-majority areas

entries.description: Global network of Christian hospitals, schools, universities, and NGOs operating in nearly every country
entries.causation_evidence: Catholic, Protestant, and Orthodox charitable organizations

entries.description: Promotion of forgiveness, reconciliation, and non-violence in many Christian traditions and movements
entries.causation_evidence: Theological traditions and modern peace/reconciliation movements

entries.description: Major role in the development of Western legal systems, human rights concepts, and abolitionist movements
entries.causation_evidence: Historical influence on common law and human rights frameworks

entries.description: Artistic, musical, architectural, and literary masterpieces inspired by Christian faith across two millennia
entries.causation_evidence: Cathedrals, Renaissance art, classical music, literature

entries.description: Emphasis on forgiveness, redemption, and the inherent dignity of every human being in core teachings
entries.causation_evidence: Core Christian theology and its historical influence
